name: Qasim ibn Abbas
title: Young Hero
shortDescription: The brave son of Hazrat Abbas (AS) who, following his father's example, sacrificed his life defending Imam Hussain (AS).
fullStory:
Qasim ibn Abbas was the valiant young son of Hazrat Abu al-Fadl al-Abbas (AS). He was raised in a home where his father was the embodiment of loyalty, zeal, and bravery.

On the day of Ashura, Qasim witnessed his father's unparalleled sacrifices to bring water to the tents, followed by his tragic martyrdom near the Alqamah stream. This immense grief doubled his resolve to fight the army of disbelief.

After his father's martyrdom, Qasim ibn Abbas drew his sword and, roaring to avenge his father and defend his uncle Imam Hussain (AS), charged into the heart of Umar Sa'd's army. With the bravery he inherited, he dealt heavy blows to the enemy.

After an epic battle, enemies surrounded him, and this Hashemite youth also attained the great honor of martyrdom through sword and spear strikes, his pure body falling alongside the other Hashemite martyrs.
quote: (In historical books:) Qasim, the son of Abbas, was martyred alongside Imam Hussain (AS).
source: Manaqib Al Abi Talib, Ibn Shahr Ashub
